image_prompts.prompt: Hook visual: Here's what science actually says about mast cells.
Context visual: Mast cells are tiny defenders in your immune system, hidden in tissues like your skin and lungs.
Escalation visual: They release chemicals like histamine during allergic reactions — but that's just the beginning.
Evidence visual: Recent studies show mast cells also play roles in wound healing and even cancer defense.
Payoff visual: Understanding these cells could change how we treat allergies and inflammatory diseases.
Cta visual: Curious about how your body protects itself? Hit that follow for more science insights!
